reasoning: As of the knowledge cutoff of November 9, 2025, search summaries indicate that no new pandemic has been declared by the WHO in 2025. Extant threats such as H5N1 lack efficient person-to-person transmission, and Mpox, though spreading, has not been classified as a pandemic. There are no explosively emerging outbreaks reported as of late 2025 that suggest a pandemic declaration is imminent. Given that there are fewer than 60 days remaining in the year and pandemics are rare events (base rate approx. 1 in 10-20 years), the likelihood of a new disease being declared a pandemic before December 31, 2025 is exceedingly low. I assign the minimum permitted probability of 0.05.
